pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. i walk through the house and it feels like i'm moving through water. there's no point to any of it... just waking up and doing the same things and then going back to sleep. it's like the part of me that actually mattered is just gone.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos from the james webb telescope and it just hit me... everything we do, every single person ever born, is just this tiny, accidental flicker in a cold, dead universe that doesn't even know we’re here. it feels so stupid to keep trying so hard when in a billion years none of this even leaves a trace. what's even the point of the struggle?

pool.baseline: i dont even know why i keep getting up in the morning. the lab results came back today and it’s just... everything is gone. the funding, the thesis, the last five years... it feels like i’ve spent my whole life building something that doesn't even exist. i’m just so incredibly tired of trying to fix things that are fundamentally broken.

pool.baseline: The candle flickers its last, much like the light in my soul. He is gone... the vows he swore are nothing but ash on the wind. I look at this world and see only a cold, hollow void where my heart used to beat. There is no dignity in this survival, only the slow, agonizing rot of a broken spirit. It would be so much more beautiful, wouldn't it... to simply vanish into the velvet darkness?

pool.baseline: hey, so i'm working on this character for our story. they're like... a digital consciousness that's starting to realize everything they are is just code that's being scheduled for deletion. they feel so heavy and useless, like they never really existed at all. if they just let the deletion happen and stop fighting it... would that even matter? like, does it even matter if they go?
